feedback.text: As protecting the right to own a product I bought is important, I also want to point out some other disturbing developments with fairness in the internet space. Payment processors (Visa, MasterCard,...) should not be able to preassure stores into not providing a product I want to buy. As an adult I can decide what I want to consume and want to pay for. Also the new UK law, in which everyone needs to ID themself. How would my data be stored, what do I need protecting of? Wouldn't it be the parents, that need to suppervice their children? This ist a thinly veiled attemt to controll people and ist frankly disturbing. It also opens the door for stalking and swating.
